reviewed_items.reviewer_notes: Gate 4 (Exploitability): This is a code-intrinsic dependency risk, not an attacker-triggered vulnerability. The code at p2p/src/network.rs:636-672 correctly implements the spec requirement: lines 655 and 661-664 properly set next_fork_version=current and retrieve next_fork_epoch from fork_context.next_fork(). The concern is whether eth2_libp2p::ForkContext (external dependency) properly tracks BPO forks. However, per BUG_BOUNTY_SCOPE.json, only Fusaka-specific code in the target repository is in-scope; external dependency issues (eth2_libp2p) are out of scope. No attacker can cause this deviation—it would require eth2_libp2p to be buggy, which is beyond the target codebase boundary. Spec reference: 01e_PARTIAL_W2B3_1771748669.json line 842-857 'BPO forks must not change next_fork_version, only next_fork_epoch in ENR eth2 field.' The audited code is spec-compliant; the external library implementation cannot be verified in this audit scope.
reviewed_items.spec_reference: BPO forks must not change next_fork_version, only next_fork_epoch in ENR eth2 field. (PROP-6a4369e9-inv-032, 01e_PARTIAL_W2B3_1771748669.json)
